title: Failure Classification and Retry Budgets · Language Lens: Dependency Outage Pause
summary: A Go 1.26 service team must use errors.Is and errors.As with bounded attempt, age, delay, cancellation, and terminal-disposition evidence; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. The learner predicts values, ownership transitions, and stop paths before seeing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the dependency outage pause increment in a typed Go failure and retry policy. Learners progress through predict…
objectives: Pause or shed broad consumer work when a shared dependency is unavailable instead of scheduling delayed retries for every message; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Apply exponential backoff and jitter without blocking consumer slots or requeueing hot loops and preserve intended availability time; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Predict Dependency Outage Pause · Theory Dependency Outage Pause
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go messaging team handles case 4058 during race-enabled integration test with duplicate delivery. Build deterministic Go evidence to classify failures and enforce attempt, age, delay, and terminal retry budgets; reject stale connection and channel generations; then make one state notification stale after recovery generation changes. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
